_comment: Example roster. Copy to roster.json and replace with real data. There is ONE archer type — every archer is the same shape. 'Different-looking' archers just have different OPTIONAL fields filled (a WA competitor is full; a casual member is sparse). Optional fields: profilePhotoUrl/profilePhotoAlt, worldArcheryId, birthDate, coaches, careerStats, performance (use null / [] when not applicable, or omit). bio is Croatian (hr). TWO photos per archer: cardPhoto* = the uniform team-grid headshot (looking at camera, ~shoulders-up); profilePhoto* = the bigger image on the individual profile page (null = front-end shows a placeholder). bowType is an ARRAY (first entry = primary, drives the grid section).

archers._notes: Full WA competitor. worldArcheryId set; has careerStats + performance. bowType is an ARRAY of every style they compete in (e.g. ['recurve','barebow']); first entry = PRIMARY bow (drives the grid section). Use [] for a coach-only entry. order = position in the grid's bow section. status defaults 'published' (visible); set 'draft' to hide while building. roles can be ['archer','coach']. coaches: list of coach names, [] if none.

Casual/young archer: worldArcheryId null, NO stats — careerStats/performance simply OMITTED (importer treats absent as []). coaches lists other archers by 'FirstName LastName' (importer links them). A minor (birthDate makes age<18) → age auto-hidden on the site.
